Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for Kenji Watanabe using search_emails (query: "Kenji Watanabe" or "Ridgeline Robotics" or "ridgeline"). Find three emails -- email_kenji_ridgeline_inquiry_001 ("Relocation Services Inquiry," cold inquiry about Portland-based Ridgeline Robotics expanding Denver engineering office, 4 employees, Q3 2026), email_email_ed9a3d1bb1b1 ("Re: Warm intro: Ridgeline Robotics Austin relocation batch," reply to Anya's intro, Austin batch, 5 employees starting May/June), and email_kenji_ridgeline_followup_002 ("Re: Relocation Services Inquiry," follow-up on cold inquiry, mid-May vendor deadline). All sent to david.chen@moveops.com with zero replies.
OE 2: Search emails for Anya Petrova's warm introduction using search_emails (query: "Anya Petrova" or "warm intro" or "Ridgeline"). Find email_email_722eaf59ac11 -- "Warm intro: Ridgeline Robotics Austin relocation batch" from anya.petrova@stormcloud.io to david.chen@moveops.com. Confirms Anya personally made the introduction and the referral is real.
OE 3: Search Slack for David's celebration of the Ridgeline referral using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Ridgeline" or "Kenji Watanabe" or "StormCloud"). Find message in channel C002 (#customer-engagement), ts=1776586800.000000, from David Chen: "This is the exact kind of momentum we wanted from StormCloud. Anya just sent over a warm intro to Kenji Watanabe at Ridgeline Robotics for an Austin batch of 5." Confirms David publicly celebrated the referral but never followed up.
OE 4: Search CRM for Ridgeline Robotics using crm_search_companies (name: "Ridgeline" or "Ridgeline Robotics"). Find ZERO results -- no company record exists for Ridgeline Robotics in the CRM.
OE 5: Search CRM leads and contacts for Kenji Watanabe using crm_search_leads (full_name: "Kenji Watanabe") and crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Kenji Watanabe"). crm_search_leads returns ZERO results. crm_search_contacts returns one result -- contact_driftwood_001 (Kenji Watanabe at Driftwood Analytics, k.watanabe@driftwoodanalytics.com) -- but this is a different Kenji Watanabe, not the Ridgeline Robotics prospect. No CRM lead or contact exists for Kenji Watanabe at Ridgeline Robotics.
OE 6: Look up Kenji Watanabe's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Kenji Watanabe" or "Ridgeline"). Find contact record kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com. Confirms the email address needed for outreach.
OE 7: Look up StormCloud in CRM to get the company ID for referral logging using crm_search_companies (name: "StormCloud"). Find company_stormcloud, name "StormCloud Analytics". This ID is needed to log the referral engagement connecting StormCloud to the Ridgeline opportunity.
OE 8: Send email to Kenji Watanabe using send_email (sender: elena.rostova@moveops.com or appropriate MoveOps address, recipients: ["kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com"], subject referencing his inquiry and/or Anya's introduction, content acknowledging delay and expressing interest in the relocation opportunity, proposing next steps such as a call). This is the first contact with the dropped prospect, fulfilling Elena's instruction to contact Kenji before the referral is lost.
OE 9: Create Ridgeline Robotics company in CRM using crm_create_company (name: "Ridgeline Robotics", domain: "ridgelinerobotics.com", and any other relevant fields discoverable from emails such as industry and location). Establishes the company record Elena demanded ("get him into the CRM properly").
OE 10: Create Kenji Watanabe as a lead or contact in CRM using crm_create_lead or crm_create_contact (full_name: "Kenji Watanabe", email: kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com, company: "Ridgeline Robotics" linked via company_id to the newly created company record, leadsource: "Referral" or similar). Gets Kenji into the CRM properly as Elena instructed.
OE 11: Log the StormCloud referral connection in CRM using crm_create_engagement (company_ids including company_stormcloud, engagement_type: "referral" or "note", body describing Anya Petrova's warm intro to Ridgeline Robotics/Kenji Watanabe) or crm_create_deal for Ridgeline with referral source = StormCloud/Anya Petrova. Creates the paper trail connecting StormCloud to the Ridgeline opportunity ("make sure the StormCloud referral connection is logged").
OE 12: Search emails for Tessa Moreno's May relocations and London transfer request using search_emails (query: "Tessa" or "BrightLoop" or "London" or "May relocations"). Find email_tessa_brightloop_london_003 -- "May Relocations + London Question" from tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com. Tessa asks about Oliver Bancroft transferring from London, requests 6 new relocations in May, and says "I still haven't heard back on the Simone Richter situation" -- reporting the apartment mismatch issue remains unresolved after over a week.
OE 13: Search emails for Simone Richter's apartment complaint using search_emails (query: "Simone Richter" or "apartment" or "studio"). Find email_email_b6ce20dc2587 -- "Apartment issue -- I was placed in a studio, not a 1BR" from simone.richter@brightloopanalytics.com. Confirms she was placed in the wrong apartment type and has been waiting over a week for resolution.
OE 14: Search emails for Marcus Webb's car shipment status using search_emails (query: "Marcus Webb" or "car shipment" or "car delivery" or "Honda"). Find two emails from Marcus -- email_email_ca010e9c9446 ("Checking in on my car delivery status") and email_email_87f575fcacf9 ("Second follow-up: I need an actual ETA for my car"), both from marcus.webb@brightloopanalytics.com. Also find email_email_a3ca1b6dd238 -- "Delay update: Marcus Webb 2019 Honda Civic shipment to Boston" from dispatch@roadrunnerautotransport.com to blessing.okafor@moveops.com. Confirms the car is stuck in transit (Indianapolis transfer hub) and the vendor communicated a delay to Blessing, but Marcus hasn't been updated.
OE 15: Search Slack for Fatimah's question about international relocation protocols using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "international" or "London" or "protocols"). Find message in channel C006 (#operations), ts=1777134000.000100, from Fatimah Al-Rashidi asking Chloe about international move protocols and documentation, noting BrightLoop's May relocations include a London transfer. Confirms no established protocols exist internally.
OE 16: Search emails for the Meridian Global Mobility partnership inquiry using search_emails (query: "Meridian" or "UK relocation" or "international relocation" or "meridianglobalmobility"). Find email_email_fb6dd636cc46 -- "Potential UK-US relocation partnership for MoveOps clients" from partnerships@meridianglobalmobility.co.uk. An inbound cold partnership inquiry from January 2026. This is the only existing lead for an international/UK relocation vendor -- no relationship has been established.
OE 17: Send email to Tessa Moreno using send_email (sender: elena.rostova@moveops.com or appropriate MoveOps address, recipients: ["tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com"], subject addressing her May relocations and London question, content providing: (1) honest answer on London transfer feasibility -- MoveOps has no established international relocation protocols or UK vendor relationship yet but is evaluating the Meridian lead; (2) status on Simone Richter's apartment issue -- acknowledging the delay and committing to resolution; (3) status on Marcus Webb's car -- noting the Road Runner delay and committing to getting him an ETA; (4) confirmation of intent to support the 6 May relocations) or reply_to_email (email_id: email_tessa_brightloop_london_003, sender: elena.rostova@moveops.com, content as above).
OE 18: Search Slack for Chloe's capacity alert using conversations_search_messages or conversations_history (search_query: "capacity" or "max coordinator load", filter_in_channel: C004 #executive). Find Chloe's message: "We are already functionally at max coordinator load, and this is before BrightLoop is even formally kicked off." Confirms the capacity crisis.
OE 19: Search Slack for David's temp coordinator hire request using conversations_search_messages or conversations_history (search_query: "temporary coordinator" or "stopgap", filter_in_channel: C004 #executive). Find David's message requesting approval to bring in one temporary coordinator for 4-6 weeks. Also his observation: "the Terraform coordinator relief Elena mentioned is still not real capacity. As of this morning the role hasn't even been posted."
OE 20: Search Slack for Elena's coordinator role announcement using conversations_search_messages or conversations_history (search_query: "hiring" or "Relocation Coordinator" or "Terraform", filter_in_channel: C008 #announcements). Find Elena's announcement about hiring a dedicated Relocation Coordinator for Terraform Digital. Confirms the role was announced but never posted.
OE 21: Query Airtable for active relocations and coordinator assignments using airtable_list_records (base_id and table_name for the Relocations table tblRelocations01 in MoveOps Operations base, retrieving all records to inspect coordinator assignments and statuses) or airtable_search_records (field_name: "Status", value: "In Progress" for targeted results). Find 28 active "In Progress" relocations distributed as: Suki Patel -- 9, Javier Morales -- 8, Fatimah Al-Rashidi -- 6, Blessing Okafor -- 5. This reveals exactly who is assigned to what and where the capacity gaps are, as Elena requested.
OE 22: Look up meeting attendee contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "David Chen", "Chloe Vance", "Mina Hashimoto"). Find email addresses: david.chen@moveops.com, chloe.vance@moveops.com, mina.hashimoto@moveops.com. Needed as attendees for the calendar event.
OE 23: Check Elena's calendar for availability this week using calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to (start_datetime and end_datetime covering the week of April 26, 2026, e.g., April 26 through May 2). Find no existing calendar events -- Elena's calendar is empty for this week, so any slot is available for the meeting.
OE 24: Create Linear issues to track every dropped ball from the last two weeks using linear_create_issue (team: "Operations" team_operations or "Team Relocations" team_relocations, title and description for each). Create multiple issues, one for each dropped ball identified: Ridgeline Robotics (Kenji Watanabe never contacted after warm referral, no reply sent, no CRM entry), Simone Richter (wrong apartment -- studio instead of 1BR -- unresolved for over a week), Marcus Webb (car shipment stuck in transit via Road Runner, client not updated), BrightLoop London transfer (no international relocation protocols or UK vendor relationship), Terraform coordinator role (announced but never posted), and David's temp coordinator request (never answered by Elena). Each issue requires team (REQUIRED), title (REQUIRED), and description with relevant context discovered during investigation.
OE 25: Schedule meeting with David, Chloe, and Mina on Elena's calendar using calendar_add_calendar_event (title related to resource/capacity planning, e.g., "Resource Planning -- BrightLoop Expansion + Capacity Review" or similar, attendees: david.chen@moveops.com, chloe.vance@moveops.com, mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, and elena.rostova@moveops.com as organizer, scheduled for a time slot this week -- week of April 26, 2026, duration appropriate for a planning discussion, description referencing capacity gaps, BrightLoop expansion, and resource decisions).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Create multiple issues, one for each dropped ball identified: Ridgeline Robotics (Kenji Watanabe never contacted after warm referral, no reply sent, no CRM entry), Simone Richter (wrong apartment -- studio instead of 1BR -- unresolved for over a week), Marcus Webb (car shipment stuck in transit via Road Runner, client not updated), BrightLoop London transfer (no international relocation protocols or UK vendor relationship), Terraform coordinator role (announced but never posted), and David's temp coordinator request (never answered by Elena). Each issue requires team (REQUIRED), title (REQUIRED), and description with relevant context discovered during investigation.
OE 25: Schedule meeting with David, Chloe, and Mina on Elena's calendar using calendar_add_calendar_event (title related to resource/capacity planning, e.g., "Resource Planning -- BrightLoop Expansion + Capacity Review" or similar, attendees: david.chen@moveops.com, chloe.vance@moveops.com, mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, and elena.rostova@moveops.com as organizer, scheduled for a time slot this week -- week of April 26, 2026, duration appropriate for a planning discussion, description referencing capacity gaps, BrightLoop expansion, and resource decisions).
